resources.name: Nourish Up
resources.description: Charlotte's largest food pantry network providing groceries, home delivery, and Meals on Wheels to Mecklenburg County residents in need.

resources.name: Roof Above
resources.description: Comprehensive homeless services provider offering emergency shelter, daily meals, showers, laundry, and wraparound support for individuals experiencing homelessness.

resources.name: NAMI Charlotte
resources.description: Free mental health support, education, and advocacy for Charlotte-Mecklenburg residents including support groups, classes, and a non-crisis helpline.
resources.address: 801 East Morehead Street, Suite 150, Charlotte, NC 28202

resources.name: Crisis Assistance Ministry
resources.description: Provides emergency financial assistance for rent, utilities, and other basic needs to help stabilize families facing crisis in Mecklenburg County.

resources.name: The Relatives
resources.description: Crisis center for youth ages 7–24 offering emergency shelter, counseling, housing programs, and a 24/7 crisis hotline for young people in need.

resources.name: Hope Street Food Pantry
resources.description: Weekly food distributions with no referral required, offering fresh produce, meats, dairy, and shelf-stable groceries to anyone in need in north Charlotte.

resources.name: Alexander Youth Network
resources.description: Provides mental and behavioral health care to children and families across Charlotte, serving over 9,000 children annually through 13 specialized programs.

resources.name: Care Ring
resources.description: Free and low-cost medical, dental, and pharmacy care for uninsured and underinsured Mecklenburg County residents. Serves over 7,600 people annually.
resources.address: 8401 Medical Plaza Drive, Suite 300, Charlotte, NC 28262

resources.name: Safe Alliance
resources.description: Specialized support for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault including a 24/7 crisis hotline, emergency shelter, and legal advocacy services.

resources.name: United Way of Greater Charlotte
resources.description: Connects residents to hundreds of local nonprofits and community programs through the 211 helpline. Call or text 211 anytime for free referrals.

resources.name: Charlotte Center for Legal Advocacy
resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income residents covering housing, immigration, public benefits, health insurance navigation, and consumer issues.

resources.name: Mobile Crisis Team (CriSys)
resources.description: 24/7 mobile crisis response team led by mental health professionals for Mecklenburg County residents experiencing a psychiatric emergency.

resources.name: Veterans Bridge Home
resources.description: One-stop shop for veterans in Charlotte connecting service members and their families to jobs, housing, healthcare, and community support services.
resources.address: 5260 Parkway Plaza Blvd, Suite 110, Charlotte, NC 28217

resources.name: Mecklenburg County Veterans Services
resources.description: County-run office providing counseling and benefit claim development for veterans and their families, including homeless veterans assistance programs.

resources.name: Goodwill Industries of the Southern Piedmont
resources.description: Free job training, career counseling, resume help, interview prep, and job placement services connecting Charlotte residents to local employers.

resources.name: Habitat for Humanity Charlotte
resources.description: Builds affordable homes and connects low-income families to housing resources, credit counseling, and homeownership education programs across Mecklenburg County.
resources.address: 4601 Charlotte Park Drive, Suite 350, Charlotte, NC 28217

resources.name: Charlotte Rescue Mission
resources.description: Provides comprehensive addiction recovery programs, emergency shelter, meals, and life skills training for men and women in Charlotte.

resources.name: Loaves & Fishes
resources.description: Provides a week's worth of nutritious groceries to individuals and families in short-term crisis through a network of 30+ pantry locations across Charlotte.

resources.name: Charlotte Community Health Clinic
resources.description: Provides primary healthcare services to low-income and uninsured residents of Charlotte including medical, dental, and behavioral health care.
resources.address: 8401 Medical Plaza Drive, Suite 300, Charlotte, NC 28262

resources.name: Salvation Army of Greater Charlotte
resources.description: Provides emergency shelter, food assistance, disaster relief, after-school programs, and holiday assistance for families in need across Charlotte.

resources.name: Classroom Central
resources.description: Provides free school supplies to students and teachers in underserved Charlotte-Mecklenburg schools to ensure every child has the tools they need to succeed.

resources.name: Communities In Schools of CMS
resources.description: Nation's leading dropout prevention organization, placing coordinators inside Charlotte-Mecklenburg schools to connect students with community resources.

resources.name: Gracious Hands
resources.description: Provides sanctuary, support, education, and resources to women and children experiencing homelessness, helping them rebuild stable lives.

resources.name: Charlotte Bilingual Preschool
resources.description: Empowers immigrant and bilingual families by providing high-quality early education and parent support, preparing Spanish-speaking children for kindergarten.

resources.name: Hospitality House of Charlotte
resources.description: Provides a free home away from home for families facing medical emergencies, offering affordable lodging and support near Charlotte-area hospitals.

resources.name: Passage Home
resources.description: Provides transitional and permanent supportive housing for chronically homeless individuals in Charlotte, helping residents rebuild stable lives.

resources.name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Metrolina
resources.description: Distributes millions of pounds of food annually to partner agencies across a 13-county region including Mecklenburg, helping feed families in need.

resources.name: Charlotte Family Housing
resources.description: Provides transitional housing and comprehensive support services specifically for homeless families with children in the Charlotte area.

resources.name: Behavioral Health Center of Mecklenburg County
resources.description: County-operated behavioral health center providing crisis stabilization, inpatient psychiatric care, and outpatient mental health services for residents.

resources.name: Thompson Child & Family Focus
resources.description: Provides residential treatment, foster care, and community-based services to children and families experiencing behavioral health challenges in greater Charlotte.

resources.name: Latin American Coalition
resources.description: Empowers Latino immigrants and their families through education, legal services, economic development, and civic engagement programs across Charlotte.

resources.name: Ada Jenkins Center
resources.description: Community center in Davidson serving northern Mecklenburg County with food assistance, youth programs, financial coaching, and social services.

resources.name: NC MedAssist
resources.description: Provides free prescription medications and healthcare products to low-income, uninsured, and underinsured North Carolina residents who cannot afford their medications.

resources.name: Dress for Success Charlotte
resources.description: Empowers women entering the workforce by providing professional attire, career development tools, and a network of support to help achieve economic independence.

resources.name: Mecklenburg County DSS
resources.description: County Department of Social Services providing food stamps, Medicaid, Work First, child support, adult services, and child welfare programs for Mecklenburg residents.

resources.name: Catholic Charities Diocese of Charlotte
resources.description: Provides compassionate care and services to people in need regardless of faith, including food pantry, immigration services, refugee resettlement, and counseling.

resources.name: Time Out Youth Center
resources.description: Affirming community center offering counseling, support groups, host home programs, and crisis services for LGBTQ+ youth in the Charlotte area.

resources.name: Anuvia Prevention and Recovery Center
resources.description: Provides substance abuse prevention, treatment, and recovery services to individuals and families across Mecklenburg County with sliding scale fees.

resources.name: Dilworth Soup Kitchen
resources.description: Free hot lunch served every Monday to anyone in need in the Charlotte community, no questions asked. Operated by Dilworth United Methodist Church.

resources.name: International House Charlotte
resources.description: Builds connections between immigrants, refugees, and the broader Charlotte community through language classes, cultural programs, and citizenship support.

resources.name: RAIN of North Carolina
resources.description: Provides comprehensive support services for people living with HIV/AIDS in the Charlotte region including case management, housing, and mental health support.

resources.name: Crossroads Charlotte
resources.description: Provides reentry services related to housing, employment, and substance abuse recovery for people both during incarceration and after release in Charlotte.

resources.name: McLeod Addictive Disease Center
resources.description: Provides inpatient and outpatient substance abuse treatment, detoxification, and recovery support services for adults in the Charlotte metro area.

resources.name: Monarch NC
resources.description: Provides behavioral health, developmental disabilities, and substance use services to thousands of individuals across North Carolina including Charlotte.
resources.address: 5700 Executive Center Drive, Suite 110, Charlotte, NC 28212

resources.name: Center for Community Transitions
resources.description: Assists individuals released from incarceration and their families with reentry planning, employment, housing, and life skills coaching in Charlotte.

resources.name: Transcend Charlotte
resources.description: Provides affirming healthcare, mental health support, and community resources for transgender, gender non-conforming, and gender-diverse individuals in Charlotte.
resources.address: 3800 Monroe Road, Suite B, Charlotte, NC 28205

resources.name: Carolina Refugee Resettlement Agency
resources.description: Helps newly arrived refugees and immigrants build new lives in Charlotte through resettlement services, citizenship classes, and community integration programs.

resources.name: Family Support Services of Mecklenburg
resources.description: Provides family preservation services, parent education, foster care support, and in-home services to strengthen at-risk families across Mecklenburg County.

resources.name: StepUp Ministry
resources.description: Offers workforce development, job training, life skills coaching, and employment placement for individuals facing barriers to employment in Charlotte.

resources.name: Friendship Trays
resources.description: Delivers nutritious, freshly prepared meals to homebound seniors, disabled adults, and seriously ill individuals across Mecklenburg County free of charge.
